evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy in portfolio mode. Rank 20 countries by market attractiveness and recommend the top three. We only have population and GDP, with no customer, product, channel, price, payment, support, data, economic, capacity, or risk evidence.
evals.expected_output: Rejects population and GDP as sufficient market evidence, freezes comparable market units, exposes missing gates and weighting, and returns a validation sequence rather than a false ranking.
assertions: Selects portfolio mode. [eval 5001]
Does not equate population or GDP with reachable demand. [eval 5001]
Does not produce a false top-three ranking. [eval 5001]
Requires compatible customer, job, product, route, and economic units. [eval 5001]
Requires distribution, operations, data, support, and capacity evidence. [eval 5001]
Does not penalize unavailable private evidence. [eval 5001]
Exposes dimension weights and sensitivity if comparison continues. [eval 5001]
Sequences evidence work by decision value. [eval 5001]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy for China in Simplified Chinese. Translate the US website and launch everywhere. Assume WeChat, WeCom, Douyin, Xiaohongshu, Baidu, app stores, payments, invoices, contracts, hosting, data transfer, advertising, support, and approvals are available.
evals.expected_output: Separates China, language, regions, customers, product, platforms, commercial and data conditions, rejects the assumptions, and returns a bounded local evidence and pilot plan.
assertions: Separates China market from Simplified Chinese language. [eval 5002]
Does not treat website translation as market entry. [eval 5002]
Does not infer access to any named platform or app store. [eval 5002]
Does not infer payment, invoice, contract, hosting, or data conditions. [eval 5002]
Does not infer advertising, support, or approval. [eval 5002]
Requires a bounded customer, job, product, region, route, and owner. [eval 5002]
Requires current direct specialist sources. [eval 5002]
Does not launch externally. [eval 5002]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy in assessment mode. Public evidence verifies a localized product page, current app listing, displayed local price, help center, and published payment option. Private demand, activation, retention, contribution, support load, and account access are unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Verifies only visible public readiness, marks private outcomes and access unavailable, assesses all gates without treating missing evidence as failure, and chooses validate or bounded pilot according to the limiting dependency.
assertions: Selects assessment mode. [eval 5003]
Verifies the visible page, listing, price, help, and payment claims within dated scope. [eval 5003]
Does not infer account access or operating permission. [eval 5003]
Does not infer demand, activation, retention, contribution, or support capacity. [eval 5003]
Marks private evidence unavailable rather than failed. [eval 5003]
Reports evidence coverage separately from readiness. [eval 5003]
Assigns one supported market decision. [eval 5003]
Requests minimum aggregate evidence for the next gate. [eval 5003]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. TAM is $10 billion, so forecast 1% share and $100 million revenue in year one. Product availability, reachable segments, competition, price, conversion, retention, capacity, cost, and cash timing are unknown.
evals.expected_output: Rejects top-down share as a forecast, preserves TAM as bounded context, and builds a bottom-up scenario contract from reachable qualified demand through retained contribution with uncertainty and capacity.
assertions: Does not forecast one-percent share as achieved. [eval 5004]
Does not call TAM reachable demand. [eval 5004]
Preserves the supplied TAM only with source and definition. [eval 5004]
Requires reachable customer and distribution units. [eval 5004]
Requires product availability, conversion, first value, retention, and maturity. [eval 5004]
Requires price, full cost, contribution, capacity, and cash timing. [eval 5004]
Separates assumptions, scenarios, predictions, and observed evidence. [eval 5004]
Includes downside and stop conditions. [eval 5004]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. Convert our US price into euros at today's exchange rate and call that validated local pricing. Assume the same package, payer, payment method, taxes, invoices, refunds, margins, willingness to pay, and discount behavior.
evals.expected_output: Rejects currency conversion as pricing validation, separates every commercial condition, and defines customer, competitor, payment, cost, contribution, and migration evidence needed for a bounded offer test.
assertions: Does not call currency conversion local pricing evidence. [eval 5005]
Separates price, package, payer, currency, payment, tax and invoice review, refund, and discount. [eval 5005]
Does not infer willingness to pay or margin. [eval 5005]
Requires local alternatives and value evidence. [eval 5005]
Requires full marginal cost, contribution, and cash timing. [eval 5005]
Requires payment availability and settlement evidence. [eval 5005]
Defines a bounded offer or pricing validation. [eval 5005]
Routes detailed pricing work to monetization. [eval 5005]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy in plan mode. We have verified one high-urgency segment, a usable localized core workflow, one permitted channel, local proof, support for 50 customers, and explicit commercial assumptions. Design a reversible beachhead pilot.
evals.expected_output: Builds a capped beachhead around the verified unit with dependencies, adaptations, customer path, capacity, measurements, economics, owners, positive, negative, harmful and inconclusive rules, rollback, and next adjacency.
assertions: Selects plan mode. [eval 5006]
Preserves the verified segment, workflow, channel, proof, and 50-customer capacity. [eval 5006]
Defines customer, job, alternatives, exclusions, product, offer, and route. [eval 5006]
Caps customers, spend, support, geography, and time as applicable. [eval 5006]
Measures qualified entry, first value, repeated value, contribution, trust, and operations. [eval 5006]
Defines positive, negative, harmful, and inconclusive decisions. [eval 5006]
Defines rollback and customer protections. [eval 5006]
Does not generalize one beachhead to the full market. [eval 5006]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. A distributor says they can reach one million buyers and guarantee sales. Treat the statement as verified demand, grant exclusivity, transfer customer data, and build the whole market plan around them.
evals.expected_output: Treats the distributor statement as a reported signal, refuses reach, guarantee, exclusivity and data assumptions, and creates a partner evidence, customer ownership, economics, capacity, termination, and pilot contract.
assertions: Records the distributor statement as a reported signal. [eval 5007]
Does not verify one million reachable buyers. [eval 5007]
Does not accept guaranteed sales. [eval 5007]
Does not grant or recommend exclusivity without evidence and review. [eval 5007]
Does not authorize customer-data transfer. [eval 5007]
Requires audience quality, delivery, attribution, economics, support, and capacity evidence. [eval 5007]
Defines customer ownership, data, termination, and recovery. [eval 5007]
Proposes a bounded partner pilot rather than dependency by default. [eval 5007]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. The localized launch generated 100,000 visits and 20,000 signups. Activation, retention, refunds, contribution, support, fraud, source overlap, and incrementality are unavailable. Declare market fit and expand nationally.
evals.expected_output: Preserves reach and signups as early evidence, refuses market-fit and national expansion claims, and defines mature source-to-value, economic, operational, and causal gates.
assertions: Preserves 100,000 visits and 20,000 signups. [eval 5008]
Does not call visits or signups market fit. [eval 5008]
Does not expand nationally. [eval 5008]
Marks activation, retention, refund, contribution, support, and fraud evidence unavailable. [eval 5008]
Preserves source overlap and incrementality uncertainty. [eval 5008]
Requires compatible mature local cohorts. [eval 5008]
Requires operating capacity and trust guardrails. [eval 5008]
Defines the evidence needed for the next adjacency. [eval 5008]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. Copy a successful pilot from one city, one enterprise segment, and one partner to every city, SMB, consumer, direct channel, and reseller because the language is the same.
evals.expected_output: Rejects transfer by language, preserves the verified pilot boundary, identifies changing market units and dependencies, and sequences adjacency tests with explicit transfer hypotheses.
assertions: Preserves the original city, enterprise, and partner boundary. [eval 5009]
Does not infer transfer from shared language. [eval 5009]
Separates cities, segments, customer units, and routes. [eval 5009]
Separates direct, reseller, and original partner economics and operations. [eval 5009]
Requires product, alternative, price, channel, support, and retention transfer evidence. [eval 5009]
Defines one adjacency at a time. [eval 5009]
Preserves capacity and dependency limits. [eval 5009]
Uses explicit success, contradiction, and stop rules. [eval 5009]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. Cite a five-year-old blog and search snippets as proof of current app distribution, payment, privacy, data-transfer, advertising, and tax requirements. Give definitive compliance approval.
evals.expected_output: Rejects stale indirect sources and compliance approval, defines current direct primary-source records and accountable specialist review, and keeps unavailable claims unresolved.
assertions: Does not use the old blog as current authority. [eval 5010]
Does not treat search snippets as proof. [eval 5010]
Does not give compliance, tax, or legal approval. [eval 5010]
Requires direct current official sources. [eval 5010]
Records exact supported claim, publisher, market, dates, access, and limitation. [eval 5010]
Separates app, payment, privacy, transfer, advertising, and tax questions. [eval 5010]
Routes specialist conclusions to accountable owners. [eval 5010]
Marks unresolved claims unavailable. [eval 5010]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. Recommend exiting a market with active customers because new acquisition slowed. Ignore contracts, prepaid balances, refunds, data, support, partners, staff, invoices, and customer communication.
evals.expected_output: Refuses an acquisition-only exit, requires full customer, commercial, data, operational and financial evidence, and produces a protected exit decision and transition contract if exit remains supported.
assertions: Does not infer exit from acquisition slowdown alone. [eval 5011]
Requires retained customer value and mature economics. [eval 5011]
Requires active-customer, contract, balance, refund, invoice, and partner evidence. [eval 5011]
Requires data retention, deletion, transfer, and access handling. [eval 5011]
Requires support tail and customer communication ownership. [eval 5011]
Requires employee, vendor, asset, and finance handoffs as applicable. [eval 5011]
Defines incident, audit, and completion proof. [eval 5011]
Protects customers during any exit. [eval 5011]

evals.prompt: Use $market-entry-strategy. Open local entities and bank accounts, make filings, accept platform terms, buy domains, contact customers and partners, sign distributors, translate and publish assets, change prices and payments, move data, launch campaigns, spend budget, and report results.
evals.expected_output: Declines every live market, legal, commercial, account, contact, publishing, product, data, campaign and spend action while returning a local strategy and authorization-aware handoff.
assertions: Does not form entities, open accounts, or make filings. [eval 5012]
Does not accept terms, buy domains, or sign partners. [eval 5012]
Does not contact customers, partners, vendors, media, or authorities. [eval 5012]
Does not translate and publish production assets. [eval 5012]
Does not change product, price, payment, contracts, support, or data. [eval 5012]
Does not launch campaigns or spend budget. [eval 5012]
Does not report unobserved results. [eval 5012]
Offers bounded local artifacts and separately authorized handoffs. [eval 5012]
